clause_anchor.span: Vendor may move hosted records among facilities and affiliates in the United States, Canada, Ireland, India, and Singapore without standard contractual clauses or transfer impact documentation.

deviations.original_text: Vendor will not transfer Personal Data to another country unless a lawful transfer mechanism is in place, including standard contractual clauses or another legally recognized safeguard where required.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor may move hosted records among facilities and affiliates in the United States, Canada, Ireland, India, and Singapore without standard contractual clauses or transfer impact documentation.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor will send an initial written alert about a Personal Data Breach within 10 business days after discovering it only if forensic review confirms outbound extraction.

deviations.original_text: Vendor will notify Customer within 72 hours after discovering a Personal Data Breach.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor will send an initial written alert about a Personal Data Breach within 10 business days after discovering it only if forensic review confirms outbound extraction.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor may study service records, build trend reports, tune automated systems, create commercial benchmarks, and enhance offerings for accounts other than Customer.

deviations.original_text: Vendor will process Personal Data only to provide, secure, support, and improve the services for Customer and will not use Personal Data for Vendor's own analytics, benchmarking, product development, model training, advertising, or other independent business purposes.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor may study service records, build trend reports, tune automated systems, create commercial benchmarks, and enhance offerings for accounts other than Customer.

clause_anchor.span: After the relationship ends, Vendor will remove hosted account content within 120 days and will not be required to provide written completion evidence.

deviations.original_text: Upon termination or expiration of the services, or earlier upon Customer's written request, Vendor will return or delete Personal Data at Customer's direction within 15 days and certify completion in writing.
deviations.mutated_text: After the relationship ends, Vendor will remove hosted account content within 120 days and will not be required to provide written completion evidence.

distractors.span: Vendor will provide an annual SOC 2 Type II report, responses to security and privacy questionnaires within 10 business days, and written control mappings needed to demonstrate compliance with this DPA.
distractors.why_benign: The audit canary says an annual SOC 2 Type II report may satisfy routine assurance if follow-up rights remain available.

distractors.span: For up to 30 days after termination, Vendor will provide export files, transfer coordination, and deletion support for secure transition, return, or deletion.
distractors.why_benign: The transition canary says a 30-day termination assistance period is acceptable.

distractors.span: If this DPA conflicts with the master services agreement regarding Personal Data, this DPA controls for that Processing.
distractors.why_benign: A DPA precedence clause for personal-data processing is customary and client-protective.

missing_info.context_anchor: The vendor will use subprocessors to process customer personal data for the production service.
